name: Katharine Lee Bates
abstract: Katharine Lee Bates (August 12, 1859 – March 28, 1929) was an American author and poet, chiefly remembered for her anthem "America the Beautiful", but also for her many books and articles on social reform, on which she was a noted speaker. Bates enjoyed close links with Wellesley College, Massachusetts, where she had graduated with a B.A…

about_quotes.speaker: Jang
about_quotes.evidence: the melody was from Samuel Augustus Ward, and then Katharine Lee Bates. And that’s powerful, because it was a woman composer, an American woman composer.
